channel.title: Teradyne Robotics — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by Teradyne Robotics (tro).

item.title: CVE-2026-8153 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Command injection in Dashboard Server interface
item.description: OS command injection in Dashboard Server interface in Universal Robots PolyScope versions prior to 5.25.1 allows unauthenticated attacker to craft commands that will execute code on the robot's OS.

item.title: CVE-2025-13819 · 6.1 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Open redirect in web server of MiR robots and MiR fleet
item.description: Open redirect in the web server component of MiR Robot and Fleet software allows a remote attacker to redirect users to arbitrary external websites via a crafted parameter, facilitating phishing or social engineering attacks.

item.title: CVE-2025-9229 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Information Disclosure in MiR robots and MiR fleet through verbose error pages
item.description: Information disclosure vulnerability in error handling in MiR software prior to version 3.0.0 allows unauthenticated attackers to view detailed error information, such as file paths and other data, via access to verbose error pages.

item.title: CVE-2025-9228 · 4.3 MEDIUM — Insufficient authorization when creating notes
item.description: MiR software versions prior to version 3.0.0 have insufficient authorization controls when creating text notes, allowing low-privilege users to create notes which are intended only for administrative users.

item.title: CVE-2025-9225 · 5.5 MEDIUM — Cross-site scripting (XSS) in MiR robots and MiR fleet
item.description: Stored cross-site scripting (XSS) in the web interface of MiR software versions prior to 3.0.0 on MiR Robots and MiR Fleet allows execution of arbitrary JavaScript code in a victim’s browser

item.title: CVE-2025-8749 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Path traversal vulnerability in MiR robot software via API requests
item.description: Path Traversal vulnerability in API Endpoint in Mobile Industrial Robots (MiR) Software Versions prior to 3.0.0 on MiR Robots allows authenticated users to extract files from the robot file system via a crafted API request.

item.title: CVE-2025-8748 · 8.8 HIGH — OS command injection in MiR robots and MiR fleet via crafted HTTP requests
item.description: MiR software versions prior to version 3.0.0 are affected by a command injection vulnerability. A malicious HTTP request crafted by an authenticated user could allow the execution of arbitrary commands on the underlying operating system.
